title: Oldenlandia mariannensis
intro: Oldenlandia mariannensis, also known as Marianna's buttonweed or Marian's buttonweed, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the family Rubiaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial effects that may help alleviate symptoms of fever, headache, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Oldenlandia mariannensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions, including infections and allergies.
taxonomy: Oldenlandia mariannensis belongs to the family Rubiaceae, commonly known as the coffee or madder family. This family comprises of approximately 7000 species, many of which are flowering plants. Oldenlandia mariannensis is a species of flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands. It is classified under the genus Oldenlandia, which contains several species of annual or perennial plants. The classification of Oldenlandia mariannensis into its correct taxonomic rank is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Gentianales, Family: Rubiaceae, Genus: Oldenlandia, Species: O. mariannensis.
